content: DSM-5-TR ADHD criteria: ≥6 symptoms (≥5 for age ≥17) of inattention and/or hyperactivity-impulsivity persisting ≥6 months to degree inconsistent with developmental level. Several symptoms present before age 12. Present in ≥2 settings (home, school/work, social). Clear evidence of interference with functioning/quality of life. Not better explained by another mental disorder. Presentation specifiers: combined, predominantly inattentive, predominantly hyperactive-impulsive. Severity specifiers: mild, moderate, severe. Partial remission specifier for those previously meeting full criteria. Common comorbidities: learning disorders, oppositional defiant disorder, anxiety, depression, substance use disorders.
